summary: Source fidelity is acceptable for the D+Q bone ClinicalTrials.gov text-snapshot candidate. The retained raw JSON hash matches the source_snapshot content hash, the source snapshot and text snapshot both use public_registry access policy, and the active source_rights record allows raw payload, normalized markdown, section index, content hash, provenance locator, and structured extraction artifact classes.

findings.claim_or_issue: The retained raw JSON SHA-256 is 3b2a5e094062fb8b16156c58dfaae082a473537da6961ebacf48b06bb19d0cb3, matching source_snapshot.content_sha256.
findings.why_it_matters: The artifact can be audited back to the exact registry payload represented by the source snapshot.
findings.recommended_action: Keep the source snapshot hash stable; create a refreshed snapshot and new text snapshot if ClinicalTrials.gov content changes.

findings.claim_or_issue: The text snapshot, linked source snapshot, and source_rights record all classify the source as public_registry for retained registry artifacts.
findings.why_it_matters: Artifact retention is controlled by both source access policy and source-rights policy, reducing rights drift.
findings.recommended_action: Continue blocking retained article full text unless a separate open reusable or repository source is classified.

findings.claim_or_issue: The text_snapshot record lists raw JSON, normalized markdown, and section-index artifact paths with SHA-256 hashes.
findings.why_it_matters: Agents can reuse artifacts while audits can detect missing or changed retained files.
findings.recommended_action: Use the normalized markdown and sections JSON as preferred inputs for future registry extraction-refresh workers.
